title: Global hunger is on the rise
source: Retrieved from World Development Indicators (SN.ITK.DEFC.ZS). Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations ( FAO ), uri: fao.org/faostat/en/#home
keyFactLabel: more people faced hunger in 2023, compared to 2015.

scenes.text: Between 2001 and 2015, global hunger fell impressively, nearly halving from 13 percent to 7.5 percent.
Since then, however, progress has stalled and even reversed. By 2023, nearly one in eleven people faced undernourishment.
This pattern is true for nearly all regions. Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, East Asia & Pacific, and Latin America & the Caribbean all saw significant improvements in the first decade since 2000...
…only for gains to slow or reverse.
In South Asia, and Latin America & the Caribbean, hunger rates have risen again…
…while progress in East Asia & Pacific has largely stalled.

The most concerning trend is in the Middle East & North Africa, Afghanistan & Pakistan and Sub-Saharan Africa where hunger has been on a continuous rise for more than a decade—from 2012 to 2023. In 2023, over 250 million people faced hunger in Sub-Saharan Africa and another 100 million people in the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan and Pakistan region.
